notes.text:
NVIDIA has released a software update for NVIDIA DGX Spark.
To protect your system, download and install the latest version of NVIDIA DGX OS from the NVIDIA DGX site.
notes.title: Summary

notes.text:
NVIDIA has released a software update for NVIDIA DGX Spark.
To protect your system, download and install the latest version of NVIDIA DGX OS from the NVIDIA DGX site.
notes.title: Security Update Notes

notes.text:
To avoid this issue, it is recommended to capture the golden image prior to the system’s initial boot, before SSH host keys and the machine-id are generated.
For systems already deployed, verify that the following files are unique per system and do not match the golden image:
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key.pub
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key.pub
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key.pub
  • /etc/machine-id
If duplicates are found, regenerate the system identifiers after booting from the SSD using the commands below.
*The Recovery image (DGX OS ISO) does not require these commands, as the BaseOS ISO image installation automatically generates these files.
Regenerate the machine-id
  • #clear the machine-id
  • sudo truncate -s 0 /etc/machine-id
  • #regenerate the machine-id
  • sudo systemd-machine-id-setup
Regenerate the ssh keys
  • rm /etc/ssh/ssh_host/*
  • ssh-keygen -A
Testing and Validation
After regeneration, confirm that all identifiers differ from those in the original golden image
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key.pub
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key.pub
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key
  • /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key.pub
  • /etc/machine-id
notes.title: Mitigation Notes

publisher.issuing_authority: NVIDIA Product Security is responsible for vulnerability handling across all NVIDIA products and services.
publisher.name: NVIDIA Product Security

document.title: Security Bulletin: NVIDIA DGX Spark - May 2026

notes.text: NVIDIA DGX OS contains a vulnerability in the factory provisioning process, where  the cloning of a base image causes identical SSH host keys to be deployed across multiple systems. The sharing of cryptographic identifiers across all similarly provisioned systems enables host impersonation or attacker-in-the-middle attacks. A successful exploit of this vulnerability might lead to code execution, data tampering, escalation of privileges, information disclosure, and denial of service.
notes.title: Vulnerability description

notes.text: Code execution, denial of service, escalation of privileges, information disclosure, data tampering
notes.title: Impacts
